title: Jump Game
description: You are given an integer array nums where nums[i] represents the maximum number of positions you can jump forward from index i. Starting at index 0, determine if you can reach the last index of the array.
hints: Track the farthest index you can reach at each step.
If the current index exceeds the farthest reachable, return false.

title: Jump Game II
description: You are given a 0-indexed array of non-negative integers nums where nums[i] represents the maximum number of positions you can jump forward from index i. Return the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index. You can assume you can always reach the last index.
hints: Use a greedy approach by tracking the current end of the jump range and the farthest reachable.
When you reach the end of the current range, increment jumps and update the range.

title: Gas Station
description: There are n gas stations along a circular route. The amount of gas at station i is gas[i]. The cost to travel from station i to station i+1 is cost[i]. Return the index of the starting gas station if you can travel around the circuit once in the clockwise direction, otherwise return -1. There is a unique solution.
hints: If the total gas is less than the total cost, no solution exists.
If you start at station i and run out of gas at station j, no station between i and j can be the start.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given a characters array tasks representing tasks a CPU needs to do, where each letter represents a different task. The CPU can only do one task at a time. Each task takes one unit of time to complete. There is a non-negative integer n that represents the cooldown period between two same tasks (there must be at least n units of time between two executions of the same task). Return the least number of units of time that the CPU will take to finish all the given tasks.
hints: The most frequent task determines the minimum length. Place the most frequent tasks first with n gaps between them.
The answer is max(len(tasks), (maxCount - 1) * (n + 1) + countOfMax).

title: Partition Labels
description: You are given a string s consisting of lowercase English letters. Partition the string into as many parts as possible so that each letter appears in at most one part. Return a list of integers representing the size of these parts.
hints: First find the last occurrence of each character.
Iterate through the string and extend the current partition's end to the last occurrence of each character seen.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i], return the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.
hints: Sort intervals by their end times.
Greedy: always keep the interval with the earliest end time and remove overlapping ones.

title: Two City Scheduling
description: A company is planning to interview 2n people. Given an array costs where costs[i] = [aCost_i, bCost_i], the cost of flying the i-th person to city A is aCost_i and to city B is bCost_i. Return the minimum cost to fly every person to a city such that exactly n people go to each city.
hints: Sort by the difference between the two costs (costA - costB).
The first n people go to city A, the rest go to city B.

title: Candy
description: There are n children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value. You are giving candies to these children subject to the following requirements: each child must have at least one candy, and children with a higher rating get more candies than their neighbors. Return the minimum number of candies you need to have to distribute to the children.
hints: Two passes: left to right, then right to left.
If a child has a higher rating than the previous, increment the candy count; similarly from the right.

title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops
description: A car travels from a starting position to a destination which is target miles east. The car starts with startFuel gallons of fuel. The car can only drive when it has fuel. There are gas stations along the way, each represented as a tuple [position, fuel] where position is the miles east from the starting position and fuel is the gallons of fuel available. The car's fuel consumption is 1 mile per gallon. Return the minimum number of refueling stops required to reach the destination, or -1 if impossible.
hints: Use a max-heap to greedily refuel at the station with the most fuel that you have passed.
At each step, if you cannot reach the next station or the destination, refuel from the heap.

solutions.python:
import heapq

class Solution:
    def minRefuelStops(self, target: int, startFuel: int, stations: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        max_heap = []
        stations.append([target, 0])
        fuel = startFuel
        stops = 0
        prev = 0
        for position, gas in stations:
            fuel -= position - prev
            while max_heap and fuel < 0:
                fuel += -heapq.heappop(max_heap)
                stops += 1
            if fuel < 0:
                return -1
            heapq.heappush(max_heap, -gas)
            prev = position
        return stops

title: IPO
description: Suppose LeetCode is starting its IPO. To sell a good price, it wants to work on some projects. Given n projects, each project i has a pure profit profits[i] and a minimum capital capital[i] is needed to start it. Initially, you have w capital. At most, you can complete k projects. Return the maximum capital you can have after completing at most k projects.
hints: Use a min-heap for projects sorted by capital and a max-heap for profits of affordable projects.
Greedily pick the most profitable project you can afford.
